children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except for division C of [ et seq.], the Secretary shall make guarantees under this or any other Act for projects, including projects receiving financial support or credit enhancements from a State energy financing institution, on such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines, after consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury, only in accordance with this section.Public Law 108–32415 U.S.C. 720

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the cost of a guarantee shall be paid by the Secretary using an appropriation made for the cost of the guarantee, subject to the availability of such an appropriation.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary has received from the borrower a payment in full for the cost of the guarantee and deposited the payment into the Treasury; or

children.label: B
children.text: a combination of one or more appropriations and one or more payments from the borrower under this subsection has been made that is sufficient to cover the cost of the guarantee.

children.heading: Source of payments
children.label: 3 Source of payments
children.text: The source of a payment received from a borrower under subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (2) may not be a loan or other debt obligation that is made or guaranteed by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: c Amount
children.text: Unless otherwise provided by law, a guarantee by the Secretary shall not exceed an amount equal to 80 percent of the project cost of the facility that is the subject of the guarantee, as estimated at the time at which the guarantee is issued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No guarantee, including a guarantee for a project receiving financial support or credit enhancements from a State energy financing institution, shall be made unless the Secretary determines that there is reasonable prospect of repayment of the principal and interest on the obligation by the borrower.

children.label: i
children.text: the strength of the contractual terms of the eligible project (if commercially reasonably available);

children.label: ii
children.text: the forecast of noncontractual cash flows supported by market projections from reputable sources, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: cash sweeps and other structure enhancements;

children.label: I
children.text: at the time of loan close; and

children.label: II
children.text: throughout the loan term after the project is completed;

children.label: v
children.text: the financial strength of the investors and strategic partners of the borrower, if applicable; and

children.label: vi
children.text: other financial metrics and analyses that are relied on by the private lending community and nationally recognized credit rating agencies, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 2 Amount
children.text: No guarantee shall be made unless the Secretary determines that the amount of the obligation (when combined with amounts available to the borrower from other sources) will be sufficient to carry out the project.

children.heading: Subordination
children.label: 3 Subordination
children.text: The obligation shall be subject to the condition that the obligation, including any reorganization, restructuring, or termination thereof, shall not at any time be subordinate to other financing.

children.heading: Interest rate
children.label: e Interest rate
children.text: An obligation shall bear interest at a rate that does not exceed a level that the Secretary determines appropriate, taking into account the prevailing rate of interest in the private sector for similar loans and risks.
30 years; or
90 percent of the projected useful life of the physical asset to be financed by the obligation (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a borrower defaults on the obligation (as defined in regulations promulgated by the Secretary and specified in the guarantee contract), the holder of the guarantee shall have the right to demand payment of the unpaid amount from the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment required
children.label: B Payment required
children.text: Within such period as may be specified in the guarantee or related agreements, the Secretary shall pay to the holder of the guarantee the unpaid interest on, and unpaid principal of the obligation as to which the borrower has defaulted, unless the Secretary finds that there was no default by the borrower in the payment of interest or principal or that the default has been remedied.

children.heading: Forbearance
children.label: C Forbearance
children.text: Nothing in this subsection precludes any forbearance by the holder of the obligation for the benefit of the borrower which may be agreed upon by the parties to the obligation and approved by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: complete, maintain, operate, lease, or otherwise dispose of any property acquired pursuant to such guarantee or related agreements; or

children.label: ii
children.text: permit the borrower, pursuant to an agreement with the Secretary, to continue to pursue the purposes of the project if the Secretary determines this to be in the public interest.

children.heading: Superiority of rights
children.label: B Superiority of rights
children.text: The rights of the Secretary, with respect to any property acquired pursuant to a guarantee or related agreements, shall be superior to the rights of any other person with respect to the property.

children.label: i
children.text: protect the interests of the United States in the case of default; and

children.label: ii
children.text: have available all the patents and technology necessary for any person selected, including the Secretary, to complete and operate the project.

children.heading: Payment of principal and interest by Secretary
children.label: 3 Payment of principal and interest by Secretary
i
children.text: the borrower is unable to meet the payments and is not in default;

children.label: ii
children.text: it is in the public interest to permit the borrower to continue to pursue the purposes of the project; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the probable net benefit to the Federal Government in paying the principal and interest will be greater than that which would result in the event of a default;

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the payment that the Secretary is authorized to pay shall be no greater than the amount of principal and interest that the borrower is obligated to pay under the agreement being guaranteed; and

children.label: C
children.text: the borrower agrees to reimburse the Secretary for the payment (including interest) on terms and conditions that are satisfactory to the Secretary.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: A Notification
children.text: If the borrower defaults on an obligation, the Secretary shall notify the Attorney General of the default.

children.label: i
children.text: such assets of the defaulting borrower as are associated with the obligation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any other security pledged to secure the obligation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall charge, and collect on or after the date of the financial close of an obligation, a fee for a guarantee in an amount that the Secretary determines is sufficient to cover applicable administrative expenses (including any costs associated with third-party consultants engaged by the Secretary).

children.label: A
children.text: be deposited by the Secretary into the Treasury; and

children.label: B
children.text: remain available until expended, subject to such other conditions as are contained in annual appropriations Acts.

children.heading: Reduction in fee amount
children.label: 3 Reduction in fee amount
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1) and subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary may reduce the amount of a fee for a guarantee under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A recipient of a guarantee shall keep such records and other pertinent documents as the Secretary shall prescribe by regulation, including such records as the Secretary may require to facilitate an effective audit.

children.heading: Access
children.label: 2 Access
children.text: The Secretary and the Comptroller General of the United States, or their duly authorized representatives, shall have access, for the purpose of audit, to the records and other pertinent documents.

children.heading: Full faith and credit
children.label: j Full faith and credit
children.text: The full faith and credit of the United States is pledged to the payment of all guarantees issued under this section with respect to principal and interest.

children.heading: Wage rate requirements
children.label: k Wage rate requirements
children.text: All laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors in the performance of construction work financed in whole or in part by a loan guaranteed under this subchapter shall be paid wages at rates not less than those prevailing on projects of a character similar in the locality as determined by the Secretary of Labor in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40. With respect to the labor standards in this subsection, the Secretary of Labor shall have the authority and functions set forth in Reorganization Plan Numbered 14 of 1950 (; 5 U.S.C. App.) and .64 Stat. 1267section 3145 of title 40

children.heading: Restructuring of loan guarantees
children.label: l Restructuring of loan guarantees
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury regarding any restructuring of the terms or conditions of a guarantee issued pursuant to this subchapter, including with respect to any deviations from the financial terms of the guarantee.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 1 Requirement
children.text: The Secretary may not make a guarantee under this subchapter until the Secretary of the Treasury has transmitted to the Secretary, and the Secretary has taken into consideration, a written analysis of the financial terms and conditions of the proposed guarantee.

children.heading: Transmission
children.label: 2 Transmission
children.text: Not later than 30 days after receiving information on a proposed guarantee from the Secretary, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transmit the written analysis of the financial terms and conditions of the proposed guarantee required under paragraph (1) to the Secretary.

children.heading: Explanation
children.label: 3 Explanation
children.text: If the Secretary makes a guarantee the financial terms and conditions of which are not consistent with the written analysis required under this subsection, not later than 30 days after making such guarantee, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate, a written explanation of any material inconsistencies.

children.heading: Request
children.label: 1 Request
children.text: If the Secretary does not make a final decision on an application for a guarantee under this subchapter by the date that is 180 days after receipt of the application by the Secretary, the applicant may request, on or after that date and not more than once every 60 days thereafter until a final decision is made, that the Secretary provide to the applicant a response described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the current status of review of the application;

children.label: B
children.text: a summary of any factors that are delaying a final decision on the application, a list of what items are required in order to reach a final decision, citations to authorities stating the reasons why such items are required, and a list of actions the applicant can take to expedite the process; and

children.label: C
children.text: an estimate of when a final decision on the application will be made.
provide assistance with the completion of applications for a guarantee under this subchapter;
conduct outreach, including through conferences and online programs, to disseminate information to potential applicants;
conduct outreach to encourage participation of supporting finance institutions and private lenders in eligible projects and projects described in .section 16517(a) of this title

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: p Coordination
children.text: In carrying out this subchapter, the Secretary shall coordinate activities under this subchapter with activities of other relevant offices with the Department.
a list of such projects, including the guarantee amount, construction status, and financing partners of each such project;
the status of each such project’s loan repayment, including interest paid and future repayment projections;
an estimate of the air pollutant or greenhouse gas emissions avoided or reduced from each such project;
data regarding the number of direct and indirect jobs retained, restored, or created by such projects;

children.label: A
children.text: technologies deployed by projects that have received guarantees that have subsequently been deployed commercially without guarantees; and

children.label: B
children.text: novel technologies that have been deployed by such projects and deployed in the commercial energy market;
the number of new projects projected to receive a guarantee under this subchapter during the next 2 years and the aggregate guarantee amount;
the number of outreach engagements conducted with potential applicants;
the number of applications received and currently pending for each open solicitation; and
any other metrics the Secretary finds appropriate.

children.heading: Conflicts of interest1So in original. Two subsecs. (r) have been enacted.1
children.label: r Conflicts of interest1So in original. Two subsecs. (r) have been enacted.1
children.text: For each project selected for a guarantee under this subchapter, the Secretary shall certify that political influence did not impact the selection of the project.

children.label: A
children.text: shall meet the requirements of ; andsection 16513(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be required to meet the requirements of .section 16513(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Partnerships authorized
children.label: 2 Partnerships authorized
children.text: In carrying out a project receiving a loan guarantee under this subchapter, State energy financing institutions may enter into partnerships with private entities, Tribal entities, and Alaska Native corporations.
